title: श्री गणेश स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश स्तोत्रम् ॥

गजाननं भूतगणादिसेवितं
कपित्थजम्बूफलचारुभक्षणम्।
उमासुतं शोकविनाशकारकं
नमामि विघ्नेश्वरपादपङ्कजम्॥१॥

नमस्ते गणनाथाय सिद्धिबुद्धिप्रदायिने।
मूषकवाहनाय च सर्वविघ्नहराय च॥२॥

एकदन्ताय शुद्धाय कपिलाय सुमङ्गल।
गणेशाय नमस्तुभ्यं ब्रह्मणस्पतये नमः॥३॥

सुमुखश्चैकदन्तश्च कपिलो गजकर्णकः।
लम्बोदरश्च विकटो विघ्ननाशो गणाधिपः॥४॥

धूम्रकेतुर्गणाध्यक्षो भालचन्द्रो गजाननः।
द्वादशैतानि नामानि यः पठेच्छृणुयादपि॥५॥

विद्यारम्भे विवाहे च प्रवेशे निर्गमे तथा।
संग्रामे सर्वकार्येषु विघ्नस्तस्य न जायते॥६॥

शुक्लाम्बरधरं देवं शशिवर्णं चतुर्भुजम्।
प्रसन्नवदनं ध्यायेत् सर्वविघ्नोपशान्तये॥७॥

अभीप्सितार्थसिद्ध्यर्थं पूजितो यः सुरासुरैः।
सर्वविघ्नहरस्तस्मै गणाधिपतये नमः॥८॥

विनायकं गुणातीतं गुणत्रयविभाविनम्।
योगिनां परमं ब्रह्म वन्दे विघ्नविनायकम्॥९॥

गणेशं पार्वतीपुत्रं शंकरस्य प्रियात्मजम्।
नमामि भक्तसुलभं लम्बोदरं गजाननम्॥१०॥

वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥११॥

॥ इति श्री गणेश स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganesha Stotram ||

Gajananam Bhutaganadisevitam
Kapitthajambuphalcharubhakshanam.
Umasutam Shokavinashkarakam
Namami Vighnesvarapadapankajam.||1||

Namaste Gananathaya Siddhibuddhipradayine.
Musakavahanaya Cha Sarvavighnaharaya Cha.||2||

Ekadantaya Shuddhaya Kapilaya Sumangala.
Ganeshaya Namastubhyam Brahmanaspataye Namah.||3||

Sumukhashchaikadantashcha Kapilo Gajakarnakah.
Lambodarashcha Vikato Vighnanasho Ganadhipah.||4||

Dhumraketurganadhyaksho Bhalchandro Gajananah.
Dvadashaittani Namani Yah Pathechchhrinuyadapi.||5||

Vidyarambhe Vivahe Cha Praveshe Nirgame Tatha.
Sangrame Sarvakaryeshu Vighnastasya Na Jayate.||6||

Shuklambaradharam Devam Shashivarnam Chaturbhujam.
Prasannavadanam Dhyayet Sarvavighnopaashantaye.||7||

Abhipsitarthasiddhyartham Pujito Yah Surasuraih.
Sarvavighnaharastasmai Ganadhipataye Namah.||8||

Vinayakam Gunatitam Gunatrayavibhavinam.
Yoginam Paramam Brahma Vande Vighnavinayakam.||9||

Ganesham Parvatiputram Shankarasya Priyatmajam.
Namami Bhaktasulabham Lambodaram Gajananam.||10||

Vakratunda Mahakaya Suryakotisamaprabha.
Nirvighnam Kuru Me Deva Sarvakaryeshu Sarvada.||11||

|| Iti Shri Ganesha Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) हाथी के मुख वाले, भूतगणों द्वारा सेवित, कैथ और जामुन के फल सुन्दरता से खाने वाले, उमा (पार्वती) के पुत्र, शोक का विनाश करने वाले विघ्नेश्वर के चरणकमलों को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

(2) गणनाथ, सिद्धि और बुद्धि प्रदान करने वाले, मूषक वाहन वाले और सभी विघ्नों को हरने वाले आपको नमस्कार।

(3) एकदन्त, शुद्ध, कपिल वर्ण, अत्यन्त मंगलकारी गणेश को नमस्कार, ब्रह्मणस्पति को नमस्कार।

(4-5) सुमुख, एकदन्त, कपिल, गजकर्ण, लम्बोदर, विकट, विघ्ननाश, गणाधिप, धूम्रकेतु, गणाध्यक्ष, भालचन्द्र, गजानन - ये बारह नाम जो पढ़ता या सुनता है;

(6) उसे विद्या आरम्भ, विवाह, प्रवेश, निकास, युद्ध और सभी कार्यों में कोई विघ्न नहीं होता।

(7) श्वेत वस्त्र धारण करने वाले, चन्द्रमा के समान कान्ति वाले, चतुर्भुज, प्रसन्न मुख वाले देव का ध्यान करना चाहिए, सम्पूर्ण विघ्नों की शान्ति के लिए।

(8) जिनकी पूजा देवता और असुर दोनों अपनी अभीष्ट सिद्धि के लिए करते हैं, उन सर्वविघ्नहर गणाधिपति को नमस्कार।

(9) गुणातीत, तीनों गुणों को प्रकट करने वाले, योगियों के परम ब्रह्म, विघ्नविनायक को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

(10) पार्वती पुत्र, शंकर के प्रिय आत्मज, भक्तों को सुलभ, लम्बोदर, गजानन गणेश को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

(11) हे वक्रतुण्ड, महाकाय, करोड़ों सूर्य के समान प्रभा वाले! हे देव, मेरे सभी कार्यों में सदा निर्विघ्नता प्रदान करें।
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles from education, marriage, travel, and all undertakings
Contains the famous 12 names of Ganesha for daily recitation
Includes the universally recited Shuklambaradharam and Vakratunda shlokas
Traditionally recited for removal of sorrow (shoka-vinashakara) and brings joy
Suitable for recitation before starting any new venture or ceremony
description: Dedicated to Lord Ganesha, the Ganesh Stotram (श्री गणेश स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Puranic Compilation. Devotees recite this prayer to include the universally recited Shuklambaradharam and Vakratunda shlokas and grant Siddhi (spiritual powers) and Buddhi (intelligence). It is also said to contain the famous 12 names of Ganesha for daily recitation. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi to invoke divine grace. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesh Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ganesh Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Contains 12 names of Ganesha and powerful shlokas for removal of all obstacles in life.
alsoKnownAs: Sankata Nashana Ganesh Stotram (most common reading)

seoFaqs.question: Which 'Ganesh Stotram' does this term most commonly refer to?
seoFaqs.answer: The simple title 'Ganesh Stotram' (or 'Ganesha Stotram') most commonly refers, in popular usage, to the Sankata Nashana Ganesha Stotram from the Narada Purana — the twelve-name Ganesha hymn beginning Pranamya shirasa devam Gauriputra Vinayakam, which is the most widely recited daily Ganesha prayer across India. However, the Hindu tradition contains many distinct Ganesha stotras: the Ganesha Atharvashirsha (Vedic-Upanishadic), the Ganesha Pancharatnam and Ganesha Bhujangam (Adi Shankaracharya), the Mayuresha Stotra, the Ganapati Talam, the Maha Ganapati Stotra of the Mudgala Purana, and stotras embedded in the Ganesha Purana. Always confirm which text is intended by the source. (Sources: Greenmesg; Bhakti Nidhi; Hinduism Outlook.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Sankata Nashana Ganesha Stotram preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sankata Nashana Ganesha Stotram — the most common 'Ganesh Stotram' in daily liturgical use — is preserved in the Narada Purana as a dialogue between sage Narada and Lord Brahma. The hymn lists twelve names of Ganesha and prescribes their recitation morning and evening for the removal of obstacles. The Purana also includes the phalashruti, which states that recitation for six months produces visible benefits and recitation for one year leads to the certain fulfilment of desires. The stotram has parallel circulation in some recensions of the Padma Purana. (Sources: Bhakti Nidhi; Hinduism Outlook; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve names of Ganesha listed in the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve names enumerated are: Vakratunda (curved trunk), Ekadanta (single tusk), Krishna-Pingaksha (with dark and tawny eyes), Gajavaktra (elephant face), Lambodara (large belly), Vikata (the formidable), Vighna-rajendra (king of obstacles), Dhumravarna (smoke-coloured), Bhalachandra (bearing the moon on the forehead), Vinayaka (foremost leader), Ganapati (lord of the ganas), and Gajanana (elephant-faced one). Each name encodes a distinct aspect of Ganesha's iconography, mythology and tantric function, and the recitation of all twelve is held to invoke his complete protective presence. (Sources: Hindu Devotional Blog; Greenmesg; Devshoppe.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the phalashruti specifically promise to reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti is unusually specific: those reciting the stotra with devotion morning and evening will be free from any kind of fear (na cha vighna-bhayam tasya); for the seekers of knowledge it will yield knowledge (vidya-arthi labhate vidyam), for the seekers of wealth it will yield wealth (dhana-arthi labhate dhanam), for those wishing children it will yield children (putra-arthi labhate putran), and for those seeking liberation it will yield moksha (moksha-arthi labhate gatim). After six months results begin to manifest; after one year all desires are fulfilled, with the assurance 'in this there is no doubt' (na atra sandeha-karanam). (Sources: Bhakti Nidhi; Devshoppe; TeluguOne.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Ganesh Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is traditionally recited twice daily, morning after bath and evening at the lighting of the lamp, facing east or north before a Ganesha murti or image. The principal weekly day is Tuesday (Mangalavara) or Friday; the principal lunar day is Sankashti Chaturthi (the fourth lunar day of the dark fortnight) and Vinayaka Chaturthi (the fourth of the bright fortnight). It is also recited at the start of any new venture, before exams, before journeys, and at the inception of any other puja or homa as the mandatory mangalacharana. Twelve recitations on Sankashti Chaturthi is a common count. (Sources: Hinduism Outlook; Hindu Devotional Blog; Greenmesg.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Ganesh Stotram differ from Ganapati Atharvashirsha?
seoFaqs.answer: Ganapati Atharvashirsha is a Vedic-Upanishadic text appended to the Atharva Veda, framed as a philosophical revelation in which Ganesha is identified with Brahman — the supreme reality (tvameva pratyaksham tattvam asi). Its recitation is understood as a Vedic adhyayana with strict rules, and its phalashruti includes both worldly and Vedantic liberation. The Sankata Nashana Ganesh Stotram is by contrast a Puranic bhakti-stotra — twelve-verse, simpler in syntax, focused on obstacle-removal and the granting of specific worldly and spiritual desires. Most devotees recite both: Atharvashirsha as a sacramental Vedic act, and Ganesh Stotram as a daily bhakti prayer. (Sources: Bhakti Nidhi; Hinduism Outlook.)
